svg.title: MN 140 Dhātuvibhaṅga Sutta — Exposition on the Elements
svg.desc: A discourse visualization of MN 140: a person deconstructed into six elements, six contact fields, eighteen mental explorations, and four foundations — each seen as not mine, not I, not my self — leading through the fire-sticks simile and the goldsmith's refined equanimity to liberation through non-fabrication.

g.text: "A person consists of six elements, six contact fields, eighteen explorations, established in four ways"
cha dhāturo ayaṁ puriso, cha phassāyatano, aṭṭhārasa manopavicāro, caturādhiṭṭhāno
"Where conceptual proliferations do not flow — the sage is said to be at peace"
yattha ṭhitaṁ maññassavā nappavattanti — muni santoti vuccati
paññaṁ nappamajjeyya · saccamanurakkheyya · cāgamanubrūheyya · santimeva so sikkheyyā
It should be seen with proper wisdom as it truly is:
‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ — taṁ netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attāti
hair, nails, teeth, skin, flesh, sinews, bones — whatever is solid, solidified, and clung to internally
bile, phlegm, pus, blood, sweat, fat, tears, urine — whatever is liquid, fluid, and clung to internally
that by which one is warmed, ages, and is consumed; that by which food is digested — heat, hot, clung to internally
upward-moving winds, downward-moving winds, winds in the belly, winds in the bowels, in-breath and out-breath
openings of the ears, nostrils, and mouth — whatever is space, vacuous, and clung to internally
Then, only consciousness remains, pure and bright — cognizes pleasant, painful, and neither-painful-nor-pleasant
SIX FIELDS OF CONTACT · CHA PHASSĀYATANĀ
The mediums through which explorations arise
With each sense base, one explores its object in three ways — conducive to pleasure, displeasure, or equanimity
cha somanassupavicārā, cha domanassupavicārā, cha upekkhupavicārā
Discernment — seeing the six elements as they truly are
Regarding that as false which has a deceptive nature
‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self’ — relinquish all attachments
netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā
Quenching — streams of conceiving no longer flow
Contact that is pleasant to experience
Contact that is painful to experience
evameva kho — in the same way
tesaṁyeva nānābhāvā vinikkhepā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati
feeling arises with contact and ceases when contact ceases
Then only equanimity remains: pure, bright, pliable, workable, and radiant
upekkhāyeva avasissati parisuddhā pariyodātā mudu ca kammaññā ca pabhassarā ca
"Like a skilled goldsmith — gold purified in the forge, freed of dross, fit to be worked into any ornament"
seyyathāpi dakkho suvaṇṇakāro … sudhantaṁ niddhantaṁ ninnītakasāvaṁ mudu ca kammaññañca pabhassarañca
↓ This equanimity could be directed to any of the four formless bases…
but does not construct or intend
Equanimity could be directed here — and last for a long time
Equanimity could be directed here — and last for a long time
Equanimity could be directed here — and last for a long time
Equanimity could be directed here — and last for a long time
So he neither fabricates nor intends them — neva taṁ abhisaṅkharoti, na abhisañcetayati
"Not constructing, not intending for existence or non-existence"
neva taṁ abhisaṅkharoti, na abhisañcetayati bhavāya vā vibhavāya vā
"Not clinging anywhere; not perturbed; personally attains complete quenching"
na kiñci loke upādiyati, anupādiyaṁ na paritassati, paccattaṁyeva parinibbāyati
"Birth is ended, the spiritual life has been lived, what had to be done has been done"
khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā ti pajānāti
Experiencing any feeling: it is impermanent; there is no holding to it; there is no delight in it
